LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT BILL LOCKYER'S, TREASURER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THIS IS A VALIDATION ACTION. CALIFORNIA COMMERCE CASINO, INC. V SCHWARZNEGGER (2007) 146 CAL. APP. 4TH, 1406, 1432. UNDER C.C.P. SECTIONS 860 AND 863, THIS ACTION MUST BE BROUGHT WHERE THE PRINCIPLE OFFICE OF THE PUBLIC AGENCY IS LOCATED; HERE, THAT IS SACRAMENTO. C.C.P. SECTION 401 APPLIES WHEN THE NORMAL RULES OF VENUE ALLOW FOR TRIAL IN SACRAMENTO. UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 401(1), PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO BRING THE ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHERE THE ATTORNEY GENERAL HAS AN OFFICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Demurrer To Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT/RESPONDENT STATE TREASURER'S OFFICE'S Demurrer To Writ Of Mandate.
APPEARANCES: Steven L. Mayer, Esq. of Howard Rice Nemerovski Canady Falk & Rabkin 399-3039 for pltfs/petitioners & opposing parties; Jeffrey I. Bedell, Deputy Atty General 916/322-6103 for deft/respondent & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
The demurrer is sustained as to causes of action one through 5 without leave to amend. Previously, this Court has held that this is a validation action and that is the basis for this ruling as well. Government Code 63048.8(d) states that any suit challenging "any matter authorized by" Government Code Title 6.7, Chapter 2, Article 6.5 is subject to the validating action procedures of Section 860. The complaint challenges the validity of Government Code 63048.63, which is one of the matters authorized by Article 6.5, and is subject to the time limitations of validation action. California Commerce Casino, Inc. v. Schwarzenegger (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 1406. The applicable 60-day (CCP 860) has long run.
The demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled because it is not clear from the face of the complaint that the defendant/respondent's sole reason for responding to the public records act request as it did is based solely on the challenged statute. Ten days to answer the 6th cause of action. Order signed in open court. (Parties to confer on setting date/briefing schedule for hearing (in January) for issuance of writ of mandate on 6th cause of action. If cannot agree, parties to submit letters with proposed dates for the Court to determine.) (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Demurrer To Writ Of Mandate

UNDER C.C.P. SECTIONS 860 AND 863, THIS ACTION MUST BE BROUGHT WHERE THE PRINCIPLE OFFICE OF THE PUBLIC AGENCY IS LOCATED; HERE, THAT IS SACRAMENTO. C.C.P.
